text.title: ads-google-campaign-setup Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for ads-google-campaign-setup. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Install and verify conversion tracking before creating any campaign that targets a conversion goal.
rule.rationale: Smart Bidding without conversion data wastes ~14 days in learning burning budget. A campaign verified firing conversions starts with useful signal immediately.

rule.statement: Default to Search campaign type for new advertisers; it provides direct intent matching and is the easiest to diagnose and optimize.
rule.rationale: Search campaigns match ads to active queries, providing clear signal about intent. Display and PMax require more data and budget to optimize effectively.

rule.statement: Always explicitly set target_search_network=False, target_content_network=False, target_partner_search_network=False for Search-only campaigns; the API does not default to Search-only like the UI recommends.
rule.rationale: API-created Search campaigns silently enable Display and Search Partners unless explicitly disabled. This wastes budget on irrelevant placements from day one.

rule.statement: One theme per ad group; 10-20 keywords per group; all keywords in a group must share the same intent and match the ad copy.
rule.rationale: Tightly themed ad groups produce higher Quality Scores because keyword-to-ad relevance improves. Mixing intents dilutes relevance and raises CPC.

rule.statement: Write at least 3 RSA headlines per ad group with 15 total headlines; include the primary keyword in at least one headline; target Ad Strength of Good or Excellent.
rule.rationale: More headlines give Google more combinations to test, improving CTR over time. Keyword inclusion in headlines raises Quality Score expected CTR component.

rule.statement: Add sitelinks (4-6), callouts (4-6), and structured snippets at account or campaign level before launch; missing extensions lower ad rank and CTR.
rule.rationale: Extensions expand ad size, increase CTR, and contribute to Quality Score. They are listed as Asset resources in API v12+; using deprecated Extension service resources returns errors.

rule.statement: Run the launch gate check and require all items to pass before flipping campaign status to ENABLED.
rule.rationale: A single missing item — no RSA, conversion tracking not firing, Display network on — can waste the entire budget before anyone notices.

rule.statement: Treat every campaign setting as explicit; never rely on API defaults matching UI recommendations.
rule.rationale: The most dangerous default: Search campaigns created via API opt into Display Network and Search Partners unless explicitly disabled. The UI defaults to Search-only. This single oversight burns budget from day one.

rule.statement: Verify budget amount_micros equals dollars * 1,000,000 before submission; budgets under 1,000,000 micros ($1/day) indicate a unit error.
rule.rationale: Budget micros confusion is the most common setup bug. A $50/day budget written as 50 instead of 50,000,000 creates a $0.000050/day budget that generates zero impressions.

rule.statement: Start with Maximize Conversions without a Target CPA target; switch to Target CPA only after accumulating 30+ conversions.
rule.rationale: Smart Bidding requires sufficient conversion history to train. Setting a Target CPA before 30 conversions forces the bidder to over-fit on sparse data and underdeliver.

rule.statement: Use Asset resources for extensions (sitelinks, callouts, images) in API v12+; the older Extension service resources return deprecated-resource errors.
rule.rationale: Google migrated extensions to the Asset model in 2022. Code written against the Extension service silently fails or returns deprecation errors without explaining the migration path.

rule.statement: Generate a campaign-spec schema (pydantic / JSON Schema) and validate before any API call — most setup failures are typos.
rule.rationale: Validation catches obvious errors before they reach the API. A spec that passes schema validation is likely to succeed on the API call.

rule.statement: Default to PAUSED status; require an explicit "go-live" step.
rule.rationale: PAUSED-first prevents accidental launch with missing elements. The go-live step is a checkpoint where humans verify everything is correct before spend begins.

rule.statement: Lock naming convention to a regex; agents producing "Test Test 1" should fail their own lint.
rule.rationale: Naming convention consistency enables quick identification and bulk operations. Enforcing it via lint prevents drift.

rule.statement: Verify conversion tracking fires (Tag Assistant or gtag debug) BEFORE creating any campaign that targets a conversion goal.
rule.rationale: Conversion tracking must be live before bidding starts. Creating a campaign with a conversion target and no tracking wastes budget during learning.

rule.statement: Start with Maximize Conversions (no Target CPA) for the first 30 conversions; switch to Target CPA only after enough data lands.
rule.rationale: Maximize Conversions is less constrained; it allows the algorithm to explore and learn the cost/conversion curve. Once enough data is available, Target CPA locks in more predictable outcomes.

rule.statement: Add all relevant assets (sitelinks, callouts, structured snippets) at account or campaign level; missing them lowers ad-rank meaningfully.
rule.rationale: Extensions are high-leverage — they increase click rates and quality score. Including them in launch is a quick win.

rule.statement: For multi-account agency setups, drive everything from a CSV / YAML so spec changes are reviewable in git before they hit accounts.
rule.rationale: Version control on specs makes audits and rollbacks possible. Agents deploying without review create untrackable changes.

rule.statement: Set start_date / end_date explicitly; default end_date is "forever" and agents that forget to set it accumulate stale forever-running campaigns.
rule.rationale: Explicit dates prevent campaigns from outliving their purpose. Campaigns with no end_date become zombie campaigns that drain budget months after their intent is obsolete.
